title: MusicSphere progresses with the Hydraulis of Dion Use Case
content:
The MusicSphere consortium met in early June in Thessaloniki, Greece, to start work on the first Use Case – the Hydraulis of Dion.

The meeting started with a trip to Katerini to visit the Greek Evangelical Church, home to the largest pipe organ installed within a church in Greece. The partners were given a guided tour and a live demonstration of the organ by Giannis Adamidis.  The visit provided consortium members with valuable insights into the preservation, performance, and cultural significance of pipe organs, which remain a central focus of the project.

Original Hydraulis of Dion (left) and the reconstruction by the International Hellenic University (right)
snippet: The MusicSphere consortium met in early June in Thessaloniki, Greece, to start work on the first Use Case – the Hydraulis of Dion.
creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: Infinity project members win two best paper awards
content:
Infinity project members have made quite an impact in the Cultural Heritage community by winning two best paper awards!

The first paper, “Cultural Heritage Survey: an Ontology Design Pattern” was awarded the Best SEMDH Paper at the Third International Workshop of Semantic Digital Humanities which was held as part of the 23rd European Semantic Web Conference (ESWC) held on the 10th-14th May in Dubrovnik, Croatia. This paper paper introduces the Cultural Heritage Survey Ontology Design Pattern (CHS-ODP), a content pattern that abstracts from specific implementations and provides a reference core for modelling survey processes within cultural heritage knowledge graphs. Further information on the Infinity website.

Three days later, a second award, Best Resource Paper, was given to “Bench4KE: Benchmarking Automated Competency Question Generation” by the ESWC which addresses the evaluation of LLM-based methods and tools that generate Competency Questions posed by Ontologists through Bench4KE, an extensible API-based benchmarking system forKnowledge Engineering automation. Further information on the Infinity website.
snippet: Infinity project members have made quite an impact in the Cultural Heritage community by winning two best paper awards!
creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: MusicSphere Special Sessions at EuroXR 2026
content:
EuroXR will take place in Valencia, Spain, from 3rd to the 6th November 2026. Several of the sister projects will participate in two special sessions organised by MusicSphere.

As a consortium partner of MusicSphere and one of the organisations leading the project’s communication and dissemination activities, EuroXR is committed to fostering collaboration among XR, Cultural Heritage and other European research initiatives. Within this framework, MusicSphere, as one of the ECHOES sister projects, will organise dedicated “XR for Cultural Heritage” Special Sessions, co-designed with participating initiatives, to showcase ongoing work, exchange experiences and strengthen collaboration across European projects. Several of the other Sister projects will also be participating in the Special Sessions which are planned as follows in the current programme:

  • Wednesday, 4th November 2026 (11:30–12:30): Pitch session focused on dissemination, networking and increasing the visibility of participating projects within the XR community.
  • Thursday, 5th November 2026 (15:00–16:30): Technical workshop dedicated to presenting ongoing developments, technologies, use cases and project results.
snippet: EuroXR will take place in Valencia, Spain, from 3rd to the 6th November 2026. Several of the sister projects will participate in two special sessions organised by MusicSphere.
creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: The first “Engagement and Collaboration” projects start
content:
ECHOES is delighted to announce that the first 12 (of 20) projects from the Cascading Grants 2nd Call “Engagement and Collaboration” have started.

The Call invited applications from Cultural Heritage institutions (CHIs) to help raise awareness of the Cultural Heritage Cloud and grow its community, to support skills development and capacity building, with a focus on small and medium-sized CHIs. A variety of approaches was encouraged such as training seminars, help desks, promotional materials along with applicants own proposals.

Here is some information about each of the projects, further details are to be found on the Second Call Awards page:

ECCCH-ACCESS
Scope: Providing training and awareness raising to the Turkish CH sector.
Geographic region(s): Turkey
Target organisations: museums, archaeological projects, libraries, researchers, and heritage professionals through established institutional and professional networks.
snippet: ECHOES is delighted to announce that the first 12 (of 20) projects from the Cascading Grants 2nd Call “Engagement and Collaboration” have started.
creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: ARXIVE Stakeholders Hub Kick-off Workshop
content:
The session will introduce the ARXIVE project’s core objectives and present the next steps for the ARXIVE Hubs: collaborative ecosystems designed to connect research, technology, and real institutional needs.

Your expertise can help shape how these Hubs develop and how they respond to challenges in the Cultural Heritage sector.

When: Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 11:00 CEST
Format: Online
Language: English
Register here: https://lnkd.in/ecV3qevY

title: AUTOMATA Archaeological Technologies Survey published
content:
The AUTOMATA consortium has published the final report of its international survey on the practices and perceptions of archaeological professionals regarding the technologies explored by the project: 3D modelling, non-destructive archaeometry, artificial intelligence and robotics.

Coordinated by the Institut national de recherches archéologiques préventives (Inrap), the survey gathered 323 responses from 42 countries between March and May 2025. Respondents were mainly active in academic research and archaeology, and showed varying degrees of familiarity with the technologies addressed, from widespread knowledge and use of 3D modelling to more limited experience with robotics.

The report highlights that 3D modelling is already widely used in archaeological practice, especially through photogrammetry, while non-destructive archaeometry is mainly applied to material characterisation, including soils and ceramics. In both areas, respondents identified lack of training, cost, access to equipment and data storage as key obstacles to wider adoption.
snippet: The AUTOMATA consortium has published the final report of its international survey on the practices and perceptions of archaeological professionals regarding the technologies explored by the project:
creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: KINETIKA’s first newsletter published
content:
The KINETIKA project have published their first newsletter which provides a comprehensive overview of their objectives and activities since starting in January.

Catch up with the goals of the project and the five pilot studies, the consortium members and kick-off meeting as well as the joint webinar held with MusicSphere.

  • Download the PDF version.
  • Subscribe to the KINETIKA newsletter.

    • About KINETIKA
    KINETIKA embraces Industry 4.0 concepts to create a groundbreaking 4D digital twin modular framework for cultural heritage objects with mechanical parts, focusing on movable structures of industrial heritage and cultural heritage (CH) masonry. The project will validate its innovations through representative case studies, including clock towers, looms and drawbridges, demonstrating the platform’s adaptability and guiding further applications across a wide range of cultural artefacts.
snippet: The KINETIKA project have published their first newsletter which provides a comprehensive overview of their objectives and activities since starting in January.
creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: ECHOES Consultation completed
content:
The publication of the ECHOES Report on identification of communities and their needs concludes the ECHOES Consultation. A booklet presenting the main results of the Consultation and the community-building strategy will be available soon.

The consultation used a mixed-method and iterative approach combining large-scale quantitative data collection through an international survey, qualitative analysis of open ended responses, and targeted validation through workshops, focus groups and  complementary consultation activities. This approach enabled both a broad mapping of the cultural heritage landscape and an in-depth understanding of community practices, needs and expectations for the Cultural Heritage Cloud.

In addition, a separate annex, Cluster profiles analysis, is also available which presents the full set of cluster profiles developed as part of the analysis. Each profile represents a distinct community configuration identified through the systematic analysis of survey responses, combining socio-professional characteristics, data practices and technological engagement.

sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: ECHOES for Portuguese-speaking Communities
content:
ECHOES and ICOM Portugal hosted a highly successful webinar “ECHOES for Portuguese-speaking Communities” on Tuesday 16th June and would like to thank all participants for their presence, interest and contributions.

The high level of participation and the quality of the discussions demonstrated the growing interest of Portuguese-speaking heritage communities in following and contributing to the development of the future European Cultural Heritage Cloud. The dialogue established during the session helped identify expectations, needs and opportunities for collaboration, all of which are essential to ensure an active and representative engagement of these communities in this process.

This meeting marked an important first step in strengthening connections between professionals, institutions and heritage networks across different Portuguese-speaking countries and regions, fostering the exchange of experiences and the development of new partnerships around the digital transformation of the sector.

To learn more and further explore opportunities for engagement, download the presentations given at the webinar.

title: Digitizing Historical Libraries in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
content:
ECHOES was mentioned extensively throughout the talk, particularly:

  • the ECHOES Consultation, as one of the most recent attempts at mapping digital cultural heritage practices and needs across Europe,
  • the diversity and fragmentation of the cultural heritage ecosystem revealed through the consultation findings,
  • the Collaborative Research Scenarios as examples of practice-oriented, cross-domain research workflows and infrastructure requirements,
  • the implications of these findings for the future development of digital cultural heritage ecosystems and cloud-based infrastructures.

    • More information: Conference website.

the ECHOES Consultation, as one of the most recent attempts at mapping digital cultural heritage practices and needs across Europe,
